title: task
description: resources.py:400 - bytes.fromhex(from_hex) accepts any valid hex string regardless of length. A 4-byte or 32-byte hex value will pass validation and be stored as the 'from' field, but the field semantically represents an 8-byte EUI-64. Failure scenario: client sends PUT /sos with {"from": "aabb"} (2 bytes); state shows {"from": "aabb"} instead of expected 16-char hex; downstream code expecting 8-byte EUI-64 may misbehave or display incorrectly.
close_reason: Fixed: Added type check for from_hex before bytes.fromhex() to prevent TypeError on non-string CBOR values. Added test case.
